item.title: Re: Karmic cops' entrapment agents?
item.content:encoded: This feels more like GOC work, tbh. Turning anomalies into pulp isn't the Foundation's style.

item.title: Karmic cops' entrapment agents?
item.content:encoded: With the Three Moons entities having this kind of anomaly-busting tech, you'd think they'd be on friendlier terms with the Foundation — possibly even collaborate to take nightmare creatures down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this well-enough; I can get behind the concept of "here's something unpleasant and no we can't do anything about it." And you sell the unpleasantness quite well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Props for implying the purpose of the new object class. I'm frustrated that the dialogue isn't funny — extradimensional boogeymen saying "Jesus fuck!" and interdimensional agents saying "Thank JALAKARA!" aren't jokes — and that only deflates the physical comedy and general humor of robot children for a boogeyman sting op. I think this would work a lot better if the whole article was separated from the Three Moons Initiative entirely and was instead tied to the faerie folk; fairy tale crime is a fun genre of urban fantasy that opens itself up to less awkward referential comedy.

item.title: The only good bogeyman is a dead bogeyman.
item.content:encoded: Regarding “Argus” - it’s a sort of step up from Cernunnos in terms of “we’re not even allowed to try and hide it, it’s in someone else’s jurisdiction now”
